summary: Pulled the live server versions of ablation_models.py and train_wikitext103_ablation.py back to local, replaced the local copies with those remote snapshots, optimized the dualq memory path to keep pre-stacked past K/V tensors instead of rebuilding them with torch.stack on every forward, and pushed the updated files back to the server.
why: The active remote code had drifted from the local workspace, and the most plausible avoidable runtime overhead was repeated Python-list traversal and per-forward restacking of past_kv despite the history count only being about 32 slots at 16 layers.
verification: python -m py_compile src/layer_depth_attention/ablation_models.py scripts/train_wikitext103_ablation.py; remote Get-Item confirmed updated timestamps after scp upload.
next: Restart a controlled dualq-sublayer vs baseline run after the current job finishes and compare step-time/eval-time rather than only total elapsed minutes.
